title: pinguicula vulgaris subsp. grandiflora
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. grandiflora, commonly known as the common butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. It is native to Europe and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce inflammation. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, which have been attributed to its health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. grandiflora are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues, bronchitis, and skin irritations.
